stemHtml: Can the approach and landing be continued below the MDA/H, if during a VOR/DME approach the flight crew can distinctly see and identify only the approach lights?

options.a: The approach may be continued and the landing may be completed provided the established visual reference is maintained.
options.b: The approach cannot be continued below the MDA/H unless an element of the threshold(markings or lights), is distinctly visible and identifiable.
options.c: For the approach to be continued below the MDA/H, in addition to the approach lights, a visual glideslope indicator should also be distinctly visible and identifiable.
options.d: The approach may be continued. The landing, however, may be completed provided an element of the threshold (markings or lights) can be identified prior to 1000 feet AGL.
